title: Trichosterigma californicum
taxonomy: Trichosterigma californicum is classified within the plant family Boraginaceae. This family includes around 1,600 species of plants commonly known as borage, forget-me-nots, and heliotropes. Trichosterigma californicum is further classified as a member of the genus Trichosterigma, which is native to the Americas. Within the genus, T. californicum is one of several species found in the southwestern region of the United States. The exact classification of T. californicum may be subject to revision as new research emerges.

intro: Trichosterigma californicum, also known as California yerba santa, is a perennial shrub native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico, belonging to the Lamiaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including respiratory problems, such as coughs and bronchitis, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Trichosterigma californicum include essential oils, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Herbal preparations made from this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to relieve respiratory congestion and promote relaxation.
